skill.name: conventions
skill.description: Loads project coding conventions and style guidance for writing, reviewing, refactoring, and documenting code consistently.
skill.summary: Apply project coding conventions during code work and reviews.
skill.author: Clouder0

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported two high severity weak cryptographic algorithm matches at SKILL.md lines 3 and 8. Both are false positives caused by ordinary descriptions of coding conventions, with no cryptographic implementation or unsafe behavior found.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Line 3 describes loading coding conventions and style guidelines. This is plain skill metadata and does not reference or implement a weak cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains only the description text for a coding convention skill. No cryptographic terms, APIs, scripts, or executable code are present.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Line 8 says the skill provides project coding conventions. This is benign documentation and does not create cryptographic or execution risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a simple prose summary of the skill purpose. There is no evidence of weak cryptography or dangerous behavior.

content.value_statement: Teams lose time when code style decisions are inconsistent across tasks and reviews. This skill loads project conventions so Claude, Codex, and Claude Code can follow the same standards.
actual_capabilities: Directs the assistant to read project convention guidance.
Supports writing new code with consistent naming and file organization.
Supports code reviews against documented project standards.
Supports refactoring decisions using existing style rules.
Highlights key areas such as testing, errors, and documentation.
limitations: Depends on the referenced convention file being available.
Does not enforce rules automatically in a linter or formatter.
Does not define detailed conventions inside the skill file.
Only grants read access, so it cannot modify files by itself.

use_cases.title: Standardize New Feature Work
use_cases.description: Load naming, file organization, error handling, and testing expectations before implementing a feature.

use_cases.title: Review Code for Consistency
use_cases.description: Compare a change against the team's documented conventions before approving or revising it.

use_cases.title: Guide Refactoring Decisions
use_cases.description: Use existing conventions to keep refactors aligned with project structure and documentation standards.

prompt_templates.title: Load the Conventions
prompt_templates.prompt: Load the project coding conventions and summarize the rules I should follow for this task.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner use before starting code work.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Conventions to a Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the project conventions while updating this feature. Call out any convention that affects naming, files, tests, or documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Review for Convention Drift
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this change against the project coding conventions. List only actionable issues and explain the relevant convention.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Convention-Aligned Refactor
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a refactor plan that follows the project conventions for file organization, errors, testing, and documentation.

output_examples.input: Review this pull request for project style consistency.
output: One test file should move next to the feature it verifies.
The error message should match the project's existing wording style.

output_examples.input: Help implement a new module using our conventions.
output: Use the existing module naming pattern.
Place the implementation and tests according to the convention file.
Add documentation only where the project standard requires it.

best_practices: Load the conventions before making broad code changes.
Ask for specific convention references during reviews.
Keep the referenced convention file current as standards change.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill as a substitute for automated linting.
Do not assume missing conventions from unrelated projects.
Do not apply conventions without checking the current project context.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It tells the assistant to load project coding conventions before writing, reviewing, or refactoring code.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it change files automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill only allows read access and provides guidance for code work.

faq.question: Does it include the full convention rules?
faq.answer: No. It points to a separate convention file that must be available in the project.

faq.question: Is it useful for code reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It helps compare code changes against project naming, testing, error handling, and documentation standards.

faq.question: What security issues were found?
faq.answer: The reported weak cryptography matches were false positives in plain text descriptions. No confirmed security issue was found.
